seoTitle: How to Permanently Delete a Slack Workspace
metaDescription: Permanently delete a Slack workspace as a Primary Owner, including backing up data first since the deletion cannot be undone.
intro: Shutting down a completed project or dissolving an old test server requires permanent deletion by the workspace's Primary Owner. This guide covers backing up your data first, then walking through the final confirmation steps.

steps.heading: Open Workspace Settings
steps.body: Click your workspace name, select Settings & Administration, then choose Workspace Settings.

steps.heading: Back up important data
steps.body: Before deleting, export any messages, files, or canvases you want to preserve, since deletion is irreversible.

steps.heading: Find the Delete Workspace section
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom of the Settings Overview page to locate the deletion option.

steps.heading: Confirm your identity
steps.body: Click 'Delete Workspace', check the confirmation box, and enter your account password to verify ownership.

steps.heading: Finalize the deletion
steps.body: Click the final 'Yes, Delete My Workspace' button to permanently remove the workspace from all connected devices.

faq.question: Can anyone delete a Slack workspace?
faq.answer: No, only a Primary Owner has permission to permanently delete an entire workspace.

faq.question: Is deleted workspace data recoverable?
faq.answer: No, once deleted, messages, files, and app integrations cannot be recovered.

faq.question: What happens to team members after deletion?
faq.answer: All members lose access immediately, and the workspace disappears from their sidebar.

faq.question: Is there a difference between deactivating my account and deleting the workspace?
faq.answer: Yes, deactivating removes just your own access, while deleting the workspace shuts it down for everyone.
